title: Upon Graciosa, Walking And Talking. (From 'Troy Town'.)
content:
When as abroad, to greet the morn,
     I mark my Graciosa walk,
     In homage bends the whisp'ring corn,
         Yet to confess
         Its awkwardness
     Must hang its head upon the stalk.

     And when she talks, her lips do heal
     The wounds her lightest glances give:--
     In pity then be harsh, and deal
         Such wounds that I
         May hourly die,
     And, by a word restored, live.
meaning:
Arthur Thomas Quiller-Couch's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Upon Graciosa, Walking And Talking. (From 'Troy Town'.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
